description: did:jwk resolution. The DID suffix is base64url(JWK); resolution strips the did:jwk: prefix, base64url-decodes, and parses the JSON as the public-key JWK. Both input DIDs and their decoded JWKs are taken verbatim from the did:jwk method specification's worked examples. The expected value is exactly the JWK the UniversalResolver yields — a hardcoded spec literal, NOT recomputed by the impl on the expected side.

spec.name: did:jwk Method Specification
spec.organization: Jeremie Miller (quartzjer) / W3C DID method registry
spec.section: Examples + Read (Resolve) algorithm (strip did:jwk: prefix, base64url-decode, parse JSON as the public-key JWK)

vector.note: The did:jwk spec's Examples section publishes a P-256 (EC) and an X25519 (OKP) worked example; both are used here. The resolver returns the decoded JWK verbatim, so kid/alg are absent (the spec examples carry none).

cases.description: did:jwk spec Examples — OKP X25519 worked example. The 'use':'enc' member is not part of the verification-key JWK shape the resolver models and is ignored on decode; the resolver yields kty/crv/x verbatim.

verification.note: DID string and decoded JWK quoted verbatim from the did:jwk spec Examples section.

cases.description: did:jwk spec Examples — EC P-256 worked example. The resolver decodes the JWK verbatim, including the y coordinate.

verification.note: DID string and decoded JWK quoted verbatim from the did:jwk spec Examples section.
